mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when policy is still moving down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a declining FEDFUNDS rate, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates tightening that raises discount rates and compresses risk appetite, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days once this threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate coupled with slowing payroll growth signals weakening household income and demand, so equity returns should be negative over the next month when labor deterioration is broad-based.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered 20d returns are positive in at least 55% of episodes despite simultaneous labor weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in retail sales reflects stronger consumer demand and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d SPX returns do not occur more often than negative returns in periods of rising RSAFS, or if the 3-year rolling correlation turns persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production confirms improving real activity and profit momentum, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when INDPRO is accelerating from its prior trend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with positive INDPRO acceleration, or if the signal loses significance across any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are weak while GDP growth is still decelerating, the market reads a broad demand slowdown that should pressure equities over the next quarter through lower cyclically sensitive earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined housing-growth condition fails to predict negative 60d returns better than chance, or if negative returns are absent in more than 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPI inflation while policy rates remain near zero implies real-rate repression and the risk of future tightening, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month when inflation runs hot.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not show lower 20d SPX returns than other episodes, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate near cyclical highs signals late-stage expansion and wage pressure, which should be followed by weaker equity returns over the next quarter as margins and policy expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward SPX returns are not negative on average when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or if the sign of the effect flips positive for more than two consecutive years.
